projectDescription: A Python library for building HTML documents programmatically using a Tkinter-inspired widget API, with an optional GUI editor.
keyFeatures: Build full HTML pages in pure Python using a simple widget-based API
Rich set of built-in elements: Button, Label, Heading, Image, Link, Frame, Script, TextNode, CustomTag
Inline text markup syntax for bold, italic, code, font-size spans, and centering
Flexbox-powered Frame layout with full CSS positioning support
Automatic HTML character escaping for security
Pretty-print HTML output via BeautifulSoup integration
Save HTML to file or stream with getHTML() / saveToFile()
Optional Tkinter GUI editor (py2htmlGUI) with visual element tree and live preview
publicApiSurface: Parent.Button(fg, bg, bd, bdradius, text, padx, pady)
Parent.Label(fg, bg, bd, bdradius, text, padx, pady)
Parent.Text(fg, bg, bd, bdradius, text, padx, pady)
Parent.Heading(level, text, fg, bg, bd, bdradius, padx, pady)
Parent.Image(src, alt, width, height, bd, bdradius)
Parent.Link(href, text, fg, bg, bd, bdradius, padx, pady, text_decor)
Parent.Frame(fg, bg, bd, bdradius, content, padx, pady, width, height, marginx, marginy, content_manager, flex_config, position, offset)
Parent.CustomTag(tag_name, content, fg, bg, bd, bdradius, padx, pady, **kwargs)
